key_insight: HVAF-sprayed Tribaloy-400 forms a denser, more protective oxide layer than detonation-sprayed coating, giving better resistance to molten solar-salt corrosion at 500 °C.
engineering_relevance: For concentrated solar power thermal-storage containers, HVAF appears to be the more reliable coating route for extending service life under long-duration nitrate-salt exposure.
method: Tribaloy-400 coated ASTM A516 steel by HVAF and detonation spraying
Exposed coatings to 60% NaNO3–40% KNO3 molten solar salt at 500 °C for up to 1000 h
Compared phase formation, elemental mapping, SEM microstructure, mass gain, and spallation
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that HVAF retained a uniform Cr–Mo–Si–O enriched surface with minimal salt penetration after 1000 hours, while DS showed deeper ingress, higher mass gain, and more spallation.
watch_item: The result is specific to this salt composition, temperature, and substrate, so coating selection should still be validated under the exact CSP operating cycle and thermal-shock conditions.
paper_title: Hot corrosion behavior of thermal sprayed using HVAF and DS techniques with Tribaloy-400 coatings for solar energy storage container
